title: Migrate EditTool, BashTool, and ApplyPatchTool to ToolArgs derive
purpose: Prove the ToolArgs derive macro works on real tools by migrating three representative tools — EditTool (simple all-required struct), BashTool (mixed required/optional with integers), and ApplyPatchTool (required + optional). Each tool's input_schema() becomes a one-line delegation to Args::json_schema(). The generated schemas must be byte-identical to the current hand-written schemas, validating that the macro is a drop-in replacement.
task: Migrate EditArgs, BashArgs, and PatchArgs to #[derive(ToolArgs)]. Add doc comments to all args struct fields matching the current schema descriptions exactly (EditArgs and PatchArgs currently lack doc comments). Replace each tool's input_schema() body with Args::json_schema(). Add round-trip tests comparing generated schemas against the known-good hand-written schemas. Do not migrate any other tools — this brief proves the pattern; bulk migration is future work.

requirements.title: Migrate EditArgs to ToolArgs derive
requirements.spec: EditArgs SHALL add #[derive(ToolArgs)] and doc comments on all three fields matching the current schema descriptions exactly. EditTool::input_schema() SHALL be replaced with EditArgs::json_schema(). The hand-written json!({...}) block SHALL be removed entirely.
acceptance: EditArgs has #[derive(Deserialize, ToolArgs)]
EditArgs::path has doc comment "Absolute path to the file to edit."
EditArgs::old_string has doc comment "Exact text to find and replace. Must match exactly once."
EditArgs::new_string has doc comment "Replacement text."
EditTool::input_schema() body is exactly: EditArgs::json_schema()
The json!({...}) block is removed from edit.rs
EditArgs::json_schema() output is byte-identical to the previous hand-written schema

requirements.title: Migrate BashArgs to ToolArgs derive
requirements.spec: BashArgs SHALL add #[derive(ToolArgs)]. Doc comments on fields SHALL match the current schema descriptions exactly. BashTool::input_schema() SHALL be replaced with BashArgs::json_schema(). Note: BashArgs has timeout: Option<u64> with #[serde(default)] — the macro must produce {"type": "integer", "minimum": 0} for the inner u64 type and omit the field from required.
acceptance: BashArgs has #[derive(Deserialize, ToolArgs)]
BashArgs::command has doc comment "Shell command line. Executed via `sh -c`."
BashArgs::timeout has doc comment "Timeout in seconds. 0 means wait forever. Defaults to 120."
BashArgs::working_dir has doc comment "Working directory for the subprocess."
BashTool::input_schema() body is exactly: BashArgs::json_schema()
The json!({...}) block is removed from bash.rs
BashArgs::json_schema() output is byte-identical to the previous hand-written schema

requirements.title: Migrate PatchArgs to ToolArgs derive
requirements.spec: PatchArgs SHALL add #[derive(ToolArgs)] and doc comments on both fields matching the current schema descriptions exactly. ApplyPatchTool::input_schema() SHALL be replaced with PatchArgs::json_schema(). The hand-written json!({...}) block SHALL be removed entirely.
acceptance: PatchArgs has #[derive(Deserialize, ToolArgs)]
PatchArgs::patch has doc comment "Unified-diff patch text. Supports single-file and multi-file patches."
PatchArgs::working_dir has doc comment "Directory to resolve relative paths in the patch against."
ApplyPatchTool::input_schema() body is exactly: PatchArgs::json_schema()
The json!({...}) block is removed from patch.rs
PatchArgs::json_schema() output is byte-identical to the previous hand-written schema

requirements.title: Add ToolArgs import to norn tools module
requirements.spec: The norn tools crate code SHALL import ToolArgs from norn::tool::ToolArgs (or via use crate::tool::ToolArgs) so that the derive is available in the tool source files. This import SHALL be added only to the files being migrated.
acceptance: edit.rs contains use crate::tool::ToolArgs (or equivalent import path)
bash.rs contains use crate::tool::ToolArgs (or equivalent import path)
patch.rs contains use crate::tool::ToolArgs (or equivalent import path)
cargo check -p norn succeeds

requirements.title: Schema equivalence tests
requirements.spec: Tests SHALL verify that the derived json_schema() output for each migrated tool is byte-identical to the known-good schema. Each test SHALL construct the expected schema as a serde_json::Value literal and assert_eq! against the derived output. These tests live alongside the existing tool tests.
acceptance: Test in edit.rs: assert_eq!(EditArgs::json_schema(), expected_schema) where expected_schema is the exact previous hand-written JSON
Test in bash.rs: assert_eq!(BashArgs::json_schema(), expected_schema)
Test in patch.rs: assert_eq!(PatchArgs::json_schema(), expected_schema)
All three tests pass with cargo test -p norn
Tests verify property names, types, required array, additionalProperties, and descriptions

requirements.title: Verify coexistence with non-migrated tools
requirements.spec: All non-migrated tools SHALL continue to use their hand-written input_schema() methods without modification. The workspace SHALL compile cleanly with a mix of ToolArgs-derived and hand-written schemas.
acceptance: cargo check --workspace succeeds with zero errors
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings produces zero errors
cargo test --workspace passes all existing tests
No changes to any tool file other than edit.rs, bash.rs, and patch.rs

boundaries: SHALL NOT migrate any tools other than EditTool, BashTool, and ApplyPatchTool
SHALL NOT change tool behavior — only the schema generation method changes, not execute(), pre_validate(), or any other method
SHALL NOT modify the Tool trait or ToolArgs derive macro — this brief consumes the macro as-is from NTM-001 through NTM-003
SHALL NOT add follow-up actions to any of the migrated tools — follow-up wiring is future work
verification: cargo check --workspace succeeds with zero errors
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings produces zero errors
cargo test --workspace passes all tests including new schema equivalence tests
git diff shows changes only in edit.rs, bash.rs, and patch.rs (no other tool files modified)
Each migrated tool's input_schema() body is a single line: Args::json_schema()
Each migrated args struct has doc comments on every field
grep -rn 'json!(' crates/norn/src/tools/edit.rs returns no matches (hand-written schema removed)
grep -rn 'json!(' crates/norn/src/tools/bash.rs returns no matches for the input_schema block
grep -rn 'json!(' crates/norn/src/tools/patch.rs returns no matches for the input_schema block
